mnemonics: CRAWL, INDEX, RANK, SERVE (CIRS) - The four pillars of web data management for a search engine.
keyPoints: Web Crawling is the process of automatically browsing and downloading web pages.
Indexing involves parsing downloaded content and building data structures (like an inverted index) for fast searching.
Ranking algorithms (like PageRank) determine the order of search results based on relevance and importance.
commonMistakes: Assuming a web crawl has complete coverage of the entire web (it's a small sample).
Ignoring the importance of handling duplicate and near-duplicate content during indexing.
Forgetting that web data is dynamic and unstructured, requiring constant crawler re-visits and content parsing.
